title: An elementary or secondary school teacher needs a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You need a work permit
If you’re an elementary or secondary school teacher on an exchange, you need a work permit to work in Canada.
- you must be taking part in a reciprocal teacher exchange program
- the majority of your work in Canada must be teaching
- you must meet the general eligibility requirements (opens in a new tab) for a work permit
You can apply for an employer-specific work permit (opens in a new tab) .
When you fill out your work permit application, select “Exemption from Labour Market Impact Assessment” as the type of work permit in the “Details of intended work in Canada” section.
If your job is classified under Training, Education, Experience and Responsibilities (TEER) category 0 or 1 of the National Occupational Classification (NOC) system, you’re eligible for 2-week application processing .
- submit an offer of employment
- pay a $230 employer compliance fee
- give you an offer of employment number
How to apply for a work permit

Thank you for your feedback.
